question_html:
Write down the appropriate preposition for the following sentences:

(a) The soldiers failed to rebel ____ the government.

(b) Education should be relevant ___the child’s need.

(c) Respond ____ your roll-call.

(d) The teacher was satisfied ____ his performance.

(e) A strong opposite is vital ______ a healthy democracy.
solution_html:
(a) against   (b) to    (c) to    (d) with  (e) to/for

Marks Distribution:

For writing the appropriate prepositions; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

question_html:
Complete the following sentences:

(a) Hardly had I reached the station ______.

(b) None but the brave ______.

(c) Work hard lest ______.

(d) A rolling stone ______.

(e) We must realize _______.
solution_html:
(a) when the train left

(b) deserve the fair

(c) you should/might fail in the exam

(d) gathers no moss

(e) that time and tide wait for none

Marks Distribution:

For completing the sentences correctly; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

[N.B: Any kind of sentence that is correct, will also get allocated marks.]

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Data Privacy in the Age of Technology’.
solution_html:
Data Privacy in the Age of Technology

In today’s digital world, personal information has become a highly valuable asset. Every online activity, whether shopping, studying, or social networking, leaves behind data that is stored and analyzed by companies, institutions, and even governments. While technology has improved communication and efficiency, it has also created new dangers such as hacking, identity theft, data leaks, and misuse of private information. These threats can harm individuals socially, financially, and emotionally. Therefore, data privacy is one of the most urgent challenges of the 21st century. To tackle this, strong legal frameworks, advanced security systems, and public awareness are necessary. Users must also be cautious about sharing information online. Ultimately, protecting data privacy is not only a technical safeguard but also a moral duty to ensure safety, dignity, and trust in the digital age.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the title “Data Privacy in the Age of Technology”: 0.5 mark

For explaining the value and uses of personal data: 01 mark

For pointing out risks such as hacking, theft, and misuse: 01 mark

For suggesting protection measures including laws, security, awareness, and user caution: 01 mark

For concluding with the importance of privacy in the digital era: 1.5 marks

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
